title: Eilistraee
bodyText:
Chaotic-good drow goddess — **daughter of Lolth** — whose whole purpose is freeing the drow from their mother's webbed underground prison: bring them topside, smell the flowers, dance in the moonlight. 9ft glossy-skinned drow, ankle-length silver hair, silver radiance. Goddess of swordwork and song. A solitary dance in her honor may turn your hair radiant silver. Art: art/eilistraee.jpg, art/eilistraee-projection.jpg

# Current truth (grill 2026-07-09, restructure adopted)
- **She is caged in the CULT POOL — the one the party is descending toward** ([[the-pool]], [[pool-wizard]]) — trapped there by her mother **Lolth**. Gods can't be blueprint-scattered; she exists only in that node. **Freeing HER is the Chapter-1 climax** ([[arc-chapter-1]]); **[[lathander|Lathander]] is in a DIFFERENT pool** (unknown, maybe topside).
- **Powered by reflected sunlight:** she is a moon goddess and goes dark when no sun shines on the moon. So when freed she comes out **FREE BUT DIM** — a weakened goddess who needs the party to **find and free Lathander** to be whole. She becomes their guide/tracker for the Ch-2 god-hunt.
- **The mark is HERS.** Eilistraee marks her chosen with **visions to defy Lolth and flee topside** (moonlight, dancing, a path out). It is **not fatal** — **Lolth hunts and kills the marked** ([[the-eight-legs]], [[the-assassin]]). That's why Varen's research finds only dead bearers ("King John had this... and died") — Lolth reached them, not the mark. **Kena amputated** to drop off the web.
- **Release mechanism — OPEN** ([[grill-queue]]): the mark as her handhold; Varen's **portent/divination** (David plays portent as "seeing possibilities and choosing between them") as the key; possibly a real **sacrifice**; possibly aid from beyond.

> [!dm] The mark is a blessing/summons, Lolth is the killer. **[[thread-orb]] is closed — the orb and voice are HER**, not Leliana. "Help find HIM, bring him back" now reads literally: she needs the party to **find Lathander's pool** — in Ch 1 they free *her*; the finding is Chapter 2.
